title: Seychelles 7-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Seychelles 7-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Seychelles, Seychelles: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve and Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion. Prices from $2851 per person (estimate).
highlights: Private island resort stay (North Island or Fregate)
Private beach picnic on Anse Lazio
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Seychelles, Seychelles — Tropical climate with consistent warmth year-round (26-31°C); the northwest monsoon (Dec-Mar) brings more rain, and the southeast monsoon (May-Sep) brings cooler, breezier, drier conditions. April-May and October-November shoulder seasons for calm seas, mild trade winds, and the best diving visibility. This itinerary blends Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve, Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion, Victoria Market (Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve, Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion, Victoria Market (Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Seychelles
itinerary.description: Land at Seychelles International Airport (SEZ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve — UNESCO-listed forest of endemic coco de mer palms. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Seychelles: Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion & Sainte Anne Marine National Park boat tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion — Boat trip to see giant Aldabra tortoises. In the afternoon, continue to Sainte Anne Marine National Park boat tour — Glass-bottom boat and snorkeling in the marine park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Sainte Anne Marine National Park boat tour

itinerary.title: Seychelles: Morne Seychellois National Park guided hike & Seychelles National Botanical Gardens
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Morne Seychellois National Park guided hike — Rainforest trails across Mahé's highest peaks. In the afternoon, continue to Seychelles National Botanical Gardens — Historic gardens with giant tortoises in Victoria. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Morne Seychellois National Park guided hike

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Praslin Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Praslin Island, a popular side trip from Seychelles. Return to Seychelles in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Anse Georgette hidden beach — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Seychelles: La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour & Aride Island Nature Reserve seabird tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour — Guided tour of the car-free island. In the afternoon, continue to Aride Island Nature Reserve seabird tour — Boat trip to a protected seabird sanctuary. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour
Aride Island Nature Reserve seabird tour

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Glass-bottom boat at Anse Volbert or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SEZ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book the Cat Cocos ferry between islands in advance during peak season
Rent a bicycle on La Digue rather than relying on the few available taxis
Bring cash for small vendors and markets, as resorts take cards but rural areas often don't
Most beaches have no facilities, so bring your own water and supplies
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen, essential to protect the coral reefs
Lightweight breathable clothing plus a light rain jacket
Sturdy sandals for granite boulder beach terrain
A dry bag for inter-island boat transfers
thingsToDo: Sainte Anne Marine National Park boat tour
Morne Seychellois National Park guided hike
La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour
Aride Island Nature Reserve seabird tour
thingsToAvoid: Touching or collecting coco de mer nuts, which are protected
Swimming in unmarked areas with strong currents
Underestimating dining costs — groceries and imported goods are pricey
Missing the last inter-island ferry, which can strand you overnight
topRestaurants: Boat House Restaurant — Creole seafood buffet ($$$)
Le Chevalier Bay — Seafood and Creole ($$)
Bravo Beach Bar — Casual beachfront grill ($$)
La Plage Beach Restaurant — Seafood and grill ($$)
instagramSpots: Vallée de Mai coco de mer palms
luxuryExperiences: Private island resort stay (North Island or Fregate)
Spa retreat overlooking the Indian Ocean
honeymoonExperiences: Private beach picnic on Anse Lazio
Couples spa on a private deck
budgetExperiences: Free public beach at Beau Vallon
Snorkel from public beach access points
localEtiquette: Greet with "Bonzour" (Creole) before starting a conversation or transaction.
Dress modestly away from beach and resort areas.
Never touch or collect coco de mer nuts or disturb giant tortoises — both are protected.
A tip of around 10% is appreciated in restaurants, though not obligatory.
Ask permission before photographing local residents.
localTransportGuide: Inter-island travel relies on Cat Cocos ferries between Mahé, Praslin, and La Digue, or short domestic flights. On La Digue, bicycles and ox-carts remain the traditional way to get around since cars are limited; Mahé and Praslin have taxis and rental cars.
bestTimeToVisit: April-May and October-November shoulder seasons for calm seas, mild trade winds, and the best diving visibility.
weather: Tropical climate with consistent warmth year-round (26-31°C); the northwest monsoon (Dec-Mar) brings more rain, and the southeast monsoon (May-Sep) brings cooler, breezier, drier conditions.
visaInformation: Seychelles is visa-free for virtually all nationalities for tourism, typically for up to three months, provided visitors hold a valid passport, a return or onward ticket, proof of accommodation, and sufficient funds — a free visitor's permit is issued on arrival.
averageFlightTime: ~11.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Seychelles?
faqs.answer: Seychelles is visa-free for virtually all nationalities for tourism, typically for up to three months, provided visitors hold a valid passport, a return or onward ticket, proof of accommodation, and sufficient funds — a free visitor's permit is issued on arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Seychelles?
faqs.answer: April-May and October-November shoulder seasons for calm seas, mild trade winds, and the best diving visibility. Tropical climate with consistent warmth year-round (26-31°C); the northwest monsoon (Dec-Mar) brings more rain, and the southeast monsoon (May-Sep) brings cooler, breezier, drier conditions.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Seychelles safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Seychelles is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Seychelles?
faqs.answer: The local currency is SCR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Seychelles is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private beach picnic on Anse Lazio experience.

reviews.author: Olivia Novak
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Seychelles was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Morne Seychellois National Park guided hike was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ethan Novak
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Seychelles National Botanical Gardens and Curieuse Island tortoise & mangrove excursion. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Daniel Santos
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Mission Lodge Ruins tour and Victoria Market (Sir Selwyn Selwyn-Clarke Market). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Fatima Nguyen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour alone was worth the trip, but combined with Mission Lodge viewpoint this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Lucas Nguyen
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Glass-bottom boat at Anse Volbert and La Digue bike & ox-cart island tour. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mohammed Fernandez
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Mission Lodge viewpoint alone was worth the trip, but combined with Vallée de Mai Nature Reserve this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sofia Silva
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Mission Lodge Ruins tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Chen Nguyen
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Seychelles National Botanical Gardens in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Omar Khan
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Beau Vallon beach and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
